key_insight: LLM agents can perform interpretable, simulation-grounded inverse design of MOFs by reasoning over chemistry and iteratively testing candidate structures.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical, low-cost route to target MOFs for adsorption, separation, and electronic-structure goals without training a separate model for each objective.
method: Agent 1 proposes human-readable design hypotheses over metal nodes, linkers, pore geometry, and functional chemistry.
Agent 2 converts hypotheses into constraints to select candidate MOFs with a metal node, organic linker, and compatible topology.
Each hypothesis is evaluated with four diagnostic beams in a closed-loop simulation workflow over ten autonomous iterations.
evidence_signal: Across six tasks, the method concentrated search on top-performing structures within 400 property evaluations and also generated de novo MOFs that outperformed random search and a genetic algorithm at about $1 per campaign.
watch_item: Performance depends on simulation fidelity and constraint quality, so interpretable agent reasoning may still miss promising structures outside the chosen hypothesis space.
paper_title: Interpretable Inverse Design of Metal-Organic Frameworks with Large Language Model Agents
